content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (iter-022)

## HIGH — B6-succ `tensorPowAdd_assoc`: FINE re-break the (ii)+(iii) residual (pre-committed gate)
The iter-021 effort-breaker split closed (i) `…_succ_reindex` (axiom-clean) and the prover banked the exact
`ihR` bridge groundwork, but (ii) interchange + (iii) coherence remain as ONE inline `sorry` @3261. The
plan-iter-021 NEXT GATE is now live: **do NOT re-dispatch a monolith prover round on the whole succ case.**
The goal is fully explicit and the obstruction is pinpointed (verified via `lean_goal`):

- **Only obstruction:** goal LHS A4 `(μ_{k,m'} ▷ L) ▷ L^m''` vs `ihR`'s first term `(μ_{k,m'} ▷ L^m'') ▷ L`
  — transpose the new `L`-factor past `L^m''`, sliding A4 past the segment-B2 braiding `β_{L,L^m''}`.
  `ihR` 2nd/3rd segments already match goal B4/D4 verbatim, so after the slide `rw [ihR]` closes the bridge.
- **Recommended FINE re-break (effort-breaker, granularity "fine"), validated by the explicit goal:**
  - (ii-a) a **named bridge lemma**: `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso (-) (L^m'')` natural against `tensorBraiding`
    — prove by routing through `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso_eq` + `tensorBraiding_eq` (iter-020 route-b
    `_trans`-helper recipe), **NOT** the `MonoidalPresheaf X` synonym side (confirmed DEAD END, iters
    015–019).
  - (ii-b) the canonical `whisker_exchange`.
  - (iii) `hexagon_forward` + `pentagon` on the four-letter word `L^k,L,L^m',L^m''` — **NO `β = id`** (both
    bracketings = SAME permutation; that is what separates assoc-∀L from comm).
- **Blueprint action (lvbc major):** the `\lean{}` pins `lem:tensorPowAdd_assoc_succ_interchange` and
  `…_succ_coherence` are DANGLING (no Lean decl). Either (a) realise them as the named helpers above, or
  (b) collapse the two blocks into one residual block. `% NOTE`s added this iter flag the state.

## MEDIUM — B7 `sectionsMul_mul_assoc` stays gated on full B6
Untouched, correctly. Unblock only when B6-succ closes (its ingredient (3) needs full `tensorPowAdd_assoc`).

## DO NOT RE-ASSIGN (blocked / no-consumer)
- 3 comm/FUTURE: `tensorBraiding_self_eq_id_of_isInvertible`, `tensorPowAdd_comm`, `sectionsMul_mul_comm`
  — `[IsInvertible L]`-gated, no consumer (`GCommSemiring` unbuilt); `sectionsMul_mul_comm` is FALSE for
  general `L`. Keep as typed sorries.
- 4 χ (`QuotScheme.lean`): cohomology-leg deferred.

## CRITICAL (tooling, 8th consecutive iter) — durable sync_leanok fix
`sync_leanok` again stripped ALL 24 closed-proof `\leanok` on `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` and corrupted
5 `\uses`-bearing blocks into build-breaking dangling LaTeX (doctor crash-level). Review repaired all 25
this iter (churn tax, now 8 iters running). **Durable fix is sync-side** — skip re-stripping decls the
prior sync verified clean, raise the per-decl `lake build` budget, or preserve the `\leanok\uses{` prefix
when stripping. The planner should escalate to the user via TO_USER (done) — no in-loop fix is in reach.

## Reusable proof patterns discovered this iter
- **`subst`-reindex helper for `eqToIso`-flanked comparison families:** `tensorPowAdd_reindex_fst`
  (`subst h; simp`) transports `μ_{a,c} → μ_{b,c}` past two `eqToIso` reindexers. Then a succ-exposure
  lemma is `rw [reindex_fst (Nat.succ_add …)]; rfl`. Reusable wherever a recursively-defined comparison
  must be read off at a reindexed argument.
- **Iso-level whiskered-`ih` bridge:** `have ihR := congrArg (fun e => tensorObjWhiskerRightIso e L) ih;
  simp only [tensorObjWhiskerRightIso_trans] at ihR` distributes the whisker into `ih`'s legs so they
  match the post-reindex goal segments verbatim — verify the match with `lean_goal` BEFORE attempting the
  close.
- **`rw [reindex_fst]` needs a trailing `rfl`** to discharge the residual `eqToIso`-pair after the subst.

## Coverage debt (optional, planner — blueprint entries)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPowAdd_reindex_fst` (private; `subst` engine of `…_succ_reindex`).
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPowAdd_succ` (private; definitional succ-clause, `rfl`).
  Both `proved:true, has_sorry:false`. SNAP convention: private helpers may stay scan-only; add `\lean{}`
  blocks only if you want them visible in the DAG.
